title: HTML Performance Optimization Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential techniques and philosophies for building high-performance web pages.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of optimizing the 'Critical Rendering Path'?
options: To ensure all JavaScript files are loaded before any HTML is parsed.
To prioritize and display the most important visible content as quickly as possible.
To encrypt all communication between the server and the browser.
To hide images from certain types of users.

questions.question: Which of these is the process of removing extra spaces, comments, and newlines from an HTML file to reduce its size?
options: Formatting
Minification
Encoding
Parsing

questions.question: What is 'Lazy Loading' (loading='lazy') primarily used for?
options: To make the browser wait for a specific time before loading any images.
To only download an image or iframe when it is about to enter the user's viewport (making the page load faster).
To only load a page when the user scrolls to the bottom.
To hide images from search engines.

questions.question: Which of these is a 'Con' of putting massive amounts of styles and scripts directly inside your HTML file (inlining)?
options: The browser cannot cache that CSS/JS for use on subsequent page visits, slowing down the overall site experience.
Search engines cannot see inline code.
Inlining makes the HTML file too small.
Inlining is not supported by any modern browser.

questions.question: Which tools should you use to identify specific performance issues and slow-loading elements on your page?
options: A regular calculator.
Google PageSpeed Insights or the Lighthouse panel in Chrome DevTools.
The browser's private browsing mode.
A simple 'ping' command.
